original: The Battle of the Teutoburg Forest ends after four days with a Germanic alliance defeating Roman forces and putting an end to expansion of the empire east of the Rhine
Benedictine abbey of Cluny founded by William, Duke of Aquitaine, in Burgundy, France, one of the greatest monasteries of Western Europe
Byzantine nobleman Isaac II Angelos kills courtier Stephanus Hagiochristophorites, triggering a revolt and the overthrow of Emperor Andronikos I Komnenos, placing Isaac on the throne
Chinese general Chih-Chung assassinates Emperor Wei Shao Wang (former Prince of Wei) in Peking and proclaims himself Regent
The Roman Catholic practice of public adoration of the Blessed Sacrament outside of Mass spreads from monasteries to parishes
William the Good becomes Earl of Holland
Lithuanian Civil War (1389-1392): the Teutonic Knights begin a five-week siege of Vilnius
Santiago, Chile, is destroyed by indigenous warriors, lead by Michimalonko
Catholic & Lutheran theology debated in Worms
An expulsion order is issued against the Moriscos of Valencia, beginning the expulsion of all of Spain's Moriscos (former Muslims and their descendants)
Peter Stuyvesant recaptures Dutch Fort Casimir from the Swedes in Delaware
Battle at Malplaquet during War of the Spanish Succession: English, Austrian and Dutch Great Alliance defeat France
French & Spanish troops under Duke of Berwick occupy Barcelona
Queen of Bohemia Maria Theresa addresses the Hungarian Parliament
Battle of Saint Cast: France repels British invasion during the Seven Years' War
Annapolis Convention on interstate commerce opens to discuss reversing protectionist trade barriers between US states
The French Blue gem (later the Hope Diamond) is stolen with other French crown jewels from Royal storehouse in Paris during Reign of Terror
France annexes the Kingdom of Piedmont
Battle of Lake Champlain: American navy defeats the Royal Navy at Plattsburgh, New York, ending the British invasion of the northern states during the War of 1812
Begun on the 6th Sept, Americans defeat British at Battle of Plattsburgh during War of 1812
1st Canadian track and field meet held at Caer Howell Grounds
Olympia Columbian is 1st newspaper published north of Columbia River
1st electric telegraph used - Merchant's Exchange, San Francisco to Pt Lobos, California
Mountain Meadows Massacre, Mormons dressed as Indians murder 120 colonists in Utah

Russo-Turkish War: Third Russian assault on Plevna, Ottoman Empire (now Pleven, Bulgaria), aided by Romanian forces mostly fails, although the Romanians take Grivitsa; up to 20,000 Russian 
 and 6,000 Turk casualties
Triple landslides bury Elm, Switzerland
American architect James Cutler patents postal mail chute for Elwood Building, Rochester, New York
Moses Hopkins, named minister to Liberia
6th America's Cup: Mayflower (NY Yacht Club) beats Galatea (Royal Northern & Clyde Yacht Club, Scotland) for 2-0 series victory
The Jewish Colonization Association is established by Baron Maurice de Hirsch
"Svoboda", oldest existing Ukrainian newspaper founded as a weekly publication by Father Hryhorii Hrushka, in Jersey City, New Jersey; expands to a daily in 1921
Bronx Gas & Electric Company opens on Frisby & Tremont Ave
First conference of the World Parliament of Religions is held in Chicago, Indian monk Swani Vivekananda gives influential speech
Shaku Soen is the first Zen teacher to visit the West (Chicago)
The first race at The Milwaukee Mile in West Allis, Wisconsin is held. The oldest major speedway in the world.
German astronomer Max Wolf rediscovers Halley's comet
First commercially successful electric bus line opens in Los Angeles
Australia invades New Britain, defeating German colonial forces
German troops conquer Kavalla, Greece
The Quebec Bridge's central span collapses during reconstruction, killing 11 men
US Marines again send troops to Honduras
Nahalal, the first moshav in Israel, is settled
Australian newspaper The Sun News-Pictorial is founded, predecessor for the Herald Sun of Melbourne
The Treaty of Kars is ratified in Yerevan, Armenia
After a single, Boston Red Sox pitcher Howard Ehmke retires next 27 New York Yankees
Bernie Neis, hits the 1,000th Dodger home run
ZR-1 (biggest active dirigible) flies over NY's tallest skyscraper, Woolworth Tower
Aloha Tower dedicated in Honolulu
Spain leaves the League of Nations due to Germany joining
Yanks' Bob Meusel ties record with 3 sacrifice flies
After losing 21 in a row to NY, the Browns win their last meeting, 6-2
First trans-Tasman crossing from Australia to New Zealand piloted by Charles Kingsford-Smith and Charles Ulm arrives in Christchurch after 14 hours 25 minutes
First-ever TV drama WGY's "The Queen's Messenger" broadcasts in the New York area, starring Izetta Jewell
San Francisco Mayor Rolph inaugurates new pedestrian traffic light system
Stromboli volcano, Sicily throws 2-ton basaltic rocks 2 miles

Polish aviators Franciszek Żwirko (36) and Stanisław Wigura (31), international 'Challenge 1932' winners, are killed when their RWD- 6 plane crashes in a forest of Těrlicko, Czechoslovakia during a storm enroute to an air meet in Prague
Antwerps Sportpaleis, the largest indoor arena in Europe opens; renovated 2010-13
A's pitcher Horace Lisenbee gives up 26 hits in a game
Battle of Kutno-pocket: Germans advance to Warsaw
British submarine Triton torpedoes British submarine Oxley
Iraq and Saudi Arabia declare war on Nazi Germany
-12] Buckingham Palace damaged by German bombs
54th US Postmaster General: Frank C. Walker of Pennsylvania takes office
Anton Mussert establishes Dutch SS
George Stibitz pioneers the first remote operation of a computer
Nazi Germany begins Operation Seelöwe (Sealion - aborted invasion of England)
Transport #31 departs with French Jews to Nazi Germany
Jewish ghettos of Minsk & Lida Belorussia liquidated
Last German Q/pirate ship sinks near Easter Island / Rapa Nui
US & Australian troops join in Salamaua, New Guinea
WWII: Allied armed forces conquer Salerno, Italy
A reconnaissance squadron of the US 5th Armored Division "Victory Division" is 1st allied force to enter Nazi-Germany
Physician Willem J. Kolff performs the first successful kidney dialysis using his artificial kidney machine in the Netherlands
1st mobile long-distance car-to-car telephone conversation
1st typesetting machine to dispense with metal type exhibited
33 die in a train crash in Coshocton, Ohio
An episode from the TV series "Dick Tracy" sparks an uproar over violent content
KSBW TV channel 8 in Salinas-Monterey, CA (NBC) begins broadcasting
WEHT TV channel 25 in Evansville, IN (CBS) begins broadcasting
20.4 cm rainfall at Brunswick, Maine (state record)
KXJB TV channel 4 in Valley City (Fargo) (NBC) begins broadcasting
Dedication of the first Temple of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in Europe, the Bern Switzerland Temple
KTVT TV channel 11 in Fort Worth-Dallas, TX (IND) begins broadcasting
Vladimir Kuts runs world record 10k (28:42.8)
Great Britain performs atmospheric nuclear test at Christmas Island
Baltimore starter Jerry Walker pitches all 16 innings as the Orioles edge the Chicago White Sox, 1-0 at Memorial Stadium
Congress passes a bill authorizing food stamps for poor Americans
Elroy Face's 22 game winning streak ends as Dodgers beat Pirates 5-4
The Young Americans for Freedom, meeting at home of William F. Buckley, Jr., promulgate the Sharon Statement
XVII Summer Olympic Games close at Stadio Olimpico in Rome, Italy
Founding of the World Wildlife Fund in Morges, Switzerland
Hurricane Carla strikes near Port O'Connor, Texas with winds of 175 mph
KVCR TV channel 24 in San Bernardino, CA (PBS) begins broadcasting
The Beatles record "Love Me Do", "PS I Love You", and a slow version of "Please, Please Me", with session player Andy White on drums; Ringo relegated to tambourine
Typhoon Gloria strikes Taiwan killing 330, with $17.5 million damage
Gillette's 20 year contract with Madison Square Garden & ABC to televise fights for free ends as Dick Tiger defeats Don Fullmer at Cleve Auditorium
Beatles' "Help!" album goes #1 & stays #1 for 9 weeks
Braves 2nd straight one-hitter against the Mets
The 1st Cavalry Division of the United States Army arrives in Vietnam
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Johnny Miller becomes 1st New York Yankee to hit a HR on his 1st MLB at bat; his 2nd HR came on his final MLB at bat in 1969
A's drop grievance filed with National Labor Relations against C Finley
Beatles' Magical Mystery Tour begins with a bus driven somewhat aimlessly around western England
Indian/Chinese border fights
US Surveyor 5 makes 1st chemical analysis of lunar material
WSRE TV channel 23 in Pensacola, FL (PBS) begins broadcasting
WUNE TV channel 17 in Linville, NC (PBS) begins broadcasting
WUNF TV channel 33 in Asheville, NC (PBS) begins broadcasting
WUNG TV channel 58 in Concord, NC (PBS) begins broadcasting
Air France Flight 1611 crashes off Nice, France, killing 89 passengers and 6 crew.
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Ford Motor Company introduces its first North American subcompact model - the Pinto

Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine members release majority of 310 hostages from the Dawson's Field hijackings, near Zarqa, Jordan; 56 hostages, mostly Jews and Israeli citizens, are held until September 25
Egypt adopts its constitution
BART begins service with a 26-mi (42-km) line from Oakland to Fremont
XX Summer Olympic Games close at the Olympiastadion in Munich, German FR
Cards beat Mets, 4-3, in 25 (7h4m), record 202 plate appearances, Félix Millán & John Milner come to bat 12 times each
Minnie Miñoso (52) bats for Chicago White Sox twelve years after his retirement as a player, he goes hitless
The last known person dies of smallpox - medical photographer Janet Parker through infection in a laboratory, in Birmingham, England
Chile adopts its constitution
Ron LeFlore (91st) & Rodney Scott (58th), set teammate steal record
Second government of Dries van Agt forms in the Netherlands
Pittsburgh running back Franco Harris runs for 118 yards in Steelers 25-21 win at Green Bay to become the only the third player in NFL history to rush for 11,000 yards
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Intl Cometary Explorer (ISEE 3) passes Giacobini-Zinner by 7900 km
Sri Lanka achieves its first Test cricket victory, defeating India by 149 runs
Dow Jones Industrial Average suffers biggest 1-day decline ever, plummeting 86.61 points to 1,792.89. 237.57 million shares traded.
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Shoot out at Jean-Bertrand Aristides' church in Haiti, 12 die
1/3 of population argues for Estonia autonomy
Lost steamship 'the Ship of Gold' SS Central America, sunk in 1857, rediscovered in waters off North Carolina by group led by Tommy Gregory Thompson using Bayesian search theory
Sports Aid-jogging to feed the world
Drexel formally pleads guilty to security fraud
KSO-AM in Des Moines Iowa changes call letters to KGGO
"La Toya: Growing Up in The Jackson Family" goes on sale
14 die in a Continental Express commuter plane crash near Houston
Atlanta Braves Kent Mercker, Mark Wohlers & Alejandro Pena pitched 1st combined no-hitter in NL, beating San Diego Padres, 1-0
NY Yankees final game at Baltimore Oriole's Memorial Stadium
Hurricane Iniki hits Kauai Hawaii; 3 die & 8,000 injured
Junxia Qu runs 1500m ladies' world record (3:50.46)
Jingyi Le swims female world record 50m freestyle
Eastern Tennessee begins using new area code 423
Ohio's Governor Voinovich proclaims "Cleveland Indians Day"
Soyuz TM-22, lands
Union Pacific Railroad purchases Southern Pacific Railroad.
After a nationwide referendum, Scotland votes to establish a devolved parliament, within the United Kingdom.
16th Commonwealth Games open in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia making Malaysia the first Asian country to host the games
Activists protest against the World Economic Forum meeting in Melbourne, Australia.
Terrorists hijack a passenger plane and crash it into the Pentagon, killing 125 people
Through extreme and coordinated effort, The Pentagon is rededicated after repairs are completed, exactly one year after the attack on the building.
Louis the Piouscrowned co-Emperor withCharlemagneof the Carolingian Empire
Ögedei, son ofGenghis Khan, is elected the Mongolian Khan, succeeding Genghis after his father's death (or 13 Sept)
Battle of Stirling Bridge, Scottish rebelWilliam Wallacedefeats the English
GeneralThomas Fairfax's New Model Army occupies Bristol after the city surrenders
Massacre of Drogheda, Ireland -Oliver Cromwellkills 3,000 royalists
Battle of Zenta: Holy League forces led by PrinceEugene of Savoydefeat an Ottoman army underMustafa IInear Zenta, Kingdom of Hungary, ending Ottoman control of large parts of Central Europe
Great Northern War:Charles XIIof Sweden stops his march to conquer Moscow outside Smolensk, marking the turning point in the war
Battle of Brandywine, Pennysylvania; Americans lose to the British, Polish soldierCasimir Pulaskisaves life ofGeorge Washington
Benjamin Franklinwrites "There never was a good war or bad peace"
Alexander Hamiltonis appointed as the first Secretary of the US Treasury
1st singing ofStephen Foster's "Oh! Susanna" (in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania)
Opera singer Jenny Lind - "The Swedish Nightingale", gives 1st US concert, at Castle Garden in New York City, promoted byP. T. Barnum
Ambrose Biercefinishes as editor of "The Wasp" magazine
After months of pursuit, generals ofMenelik IIof Ethiopia capture Gaki Sherocho, the last king of Kaffa, bringing an end to that ancient kingdom
President of TransvaalPaul Krugercrosses border into Mozambique
Mahatma Gandhicoins the term "Satyagraha" to characterize the Non-Violence movement in South Africa

Father of the Blues,W. C. Handy, publishes his most famous composition, "St. Louis Blues"
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthhits 50th home run during his MLB record 60 HR season in New York's 6-2 loss to St. Louis Browns at Yankee Stadium
Philadelphia AthleticsTy Cobbpops out in his last MLB at bat in 5-3 loss to Yankees in New York
Charles Lindbergh, claims the "British, Jewish and Roosevelt administration" are trying to get the US into World War II

Construction of the Pentagon begins in Arlington County, Virginia (completed Jan 15, 1943). Designed by architect George Bergstrom and built by contractor John McShain, construction was overseen byLeslie Groves.
FDRorders US Navy that any Axis ship found in American waters be shot at on sight
Enid Blytonpublishes "Five on a Treasure Island" first of her "Famous Five" children's novels, start of one of the best-selling children's series ever with over 100 million sold
Hideki Tojo, Japanese Prime Minister during most of World War II, attempts suicide rather than face war crimes tribunal but fails - later he is hanged
Florence Chadwickbecomes the first woman to swim the English Channel from England to France, taking 16 hours and 19 minutes
Igor Stravinsky's opera "The Rake's Progress", with libretto by W. H. Auden and Chester Kallman, premieres at the Teatro La Fenice in Venice, Italy
West German ChancellorKonrad Adenauersigns a reparation pact for the Jewish people
Juan Manuel Fango in a Mercedes wins season ending Italian Grand Prix at Monza to take his third Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by 17 points from EnglishmanStirling Moss
Cincinnati Reds outfielderFrank Robinsonties rookie record with his 38th HR

American rock guitar legendJimi Hendrixgives what becomes his final interview with NME's Keith Allston, in England

Egyptian PresidentHosni Mubarakreceives Israeli Prime MinisterShimon Peres
46th Emmy Awards: Frasier, Picket Fences andKelsey Grammerwin
Independent counselKen Starrsends a report to the U.S. Congress accusing PresidentBill Clintonof 11 possible impeachable offenses.
Attempt by passengers and crew ofUnited Airlines Flight 93to retake control of their hijacked plane from terrorists causes the plane to crash in a Pennsylvania field, killing all 44 people on board
Jay-Zreleases his album "Blueprint" featuring contributions by Eminem andKanye West
Michelangelobegins sculpting the 12 Apostles for the Cathedral of Florence, over-life-sized marble statues of which only 1 was partly finished, that of St. Matthew
First true self-service supermarket, Clarence Saunders' "Piggly Wiggly," opens in Memphis, Tennessee
The Atari 2600, originally known as the Atari Video Computer System (Atari VCS) is released in North America, revolutionizing the video game industry
Two passenger planes hijacked by Al Qaeda terrorists crash into New York's World Trade Center towers, causing the collapse of both and the deaths of 2,606 people
